mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate indicates a healthy labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, which in turn leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 consistently produces negative 20-day returns when UNRATE is below 5% over any 3-year rolling period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where RSAFS growth exceeds the trigger over a 5-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is low and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is also low, it signals an accommodative monetary environment with stable prices, encouraging investment and leading to positive equity market performance over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are consistently negative or flat when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL meet the low-level conditions over any 4-year rolling period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, a strong rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates increasing manufacturing activity and corporate profitability, which drives S&P 500 returns higher over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances during identified recovery periods, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong construction activity and consumer confidence, which positively impacts the broader economy and corporate earnings, leading to higher S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of instances when HOUST is above the specified threshold over any 3-year rolling period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading to expectations of tighter monetary policy, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds the trigger over any 5-year rolling period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with robust retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a healthy consumer base with both income and willingness to spend, which is a strong positive for corporate earnings and equity markets over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 45% of instances where both PAYEMS and RSAFS growth conditions are met over any 4-year rolling period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) signifies a robust overall economy, leading to higher corporate profits and investor confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of instances when GDPC1 growth exceeds the trigger over any 5-year rolling period, the hypothesis is rejected.
